title: Brooks Lab Western Blotting Protocol
author: Brooks Lab University of California
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcsmiwc6

steps.title: Make 100 mL, RIPA w/o protease...
steps.instruction: Make 100 mL, RIPA w/o protease inhibitor , cover, and freeze in 10 ml aliquots at -20 °C . OR Use pre-made Pierce RIPA buffer, 100 ml, which is aliquoted in 10 mL aliquots and stored in -20 °C

steps.title: Before use, thaw and add 1...
steps.instruction: Before use, thaw and add 1 tablet of protease inhibitor (PI) per 10 ml aliquot . (Roche cOmplete ™ , Mini Protease Inhibitor Cocktail).

steps.title: Always keep everything on ice, unless otherwise indicated
steps.instruction: Always keep everything on ice, unless otherwise indicated. Wash confluent 10 cm plate of cells 2X in ice cold PBS.

steps.title: Add 1 mL, cold RIPA with...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL, cold RIPA with PI to cells, and scrape the cells to remove them from the dish. Transfer to a pre-chilled 2 ml tube On ice .

steps.title: Incubate On ice 00:00:20 , with periodic vortexing
steps.instruction: Incubate On ice 00:00:20 , with periodic vortexing.

steps.title: Pellet the insoluble material by spinning...
steps.instruction: Pellet the insoluble material by spinning at max speed in refrigerated ( 4 °C ) microcentrifuge 00:00:10 .

steps.title: Transfer supernatant to a clean LoBind Protein 1
steps.instruction: Transfer supernatant to a clean LoBind Protein 1.5 ml tube as lysate. To avoid multiple freeze-thaw cycles, make aliquots (generally 200 µL each). Additional Sonicator or BioEruptor sonication step often needed here for more complete nucleic acid removal. See below.

steps.title: Store lysates aliquots at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Store lysates aliquots at -80 °C .

steps.title: Place samples in a 1
steps.instruction: Place samples in a 1.5 ml LoBind Protein microfuge tube and prepare a volume balance tube.

steps.title: Bring a timer, ear cuffs, On...
steps.instruction: Bring a timer, ear cuffs, On ice samples, and balance to second floor to Kamakaka Lab to get the plastic tube adaptor (in back of second drawer in 2nd bay from the back of the lab.)

steps.title: Sonicator is in cold room down the hall
steps.instruction: Sonicator is in cold room down the hall. Put the outflow tube up on the shelf and fill glass reservoir with water to top of white line.

steps.title: Sonicator settings
steps.instruction: Sonicator settings: timer on hold, use max setting (10), constant, then flip on.

steps.title: Hold tubes in the adaptor in...
steps.instruction: Hold tubes in the adaptor in the sonicating water bath for 00:00:30 .

steps.title: Place tubes On ice to cool for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Place tubes On ice to cool for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Repeat the 30’ sonication (= total...
steps.instruction: Repeat the 30’ sonication (= total of two rounds on max at 00:00:30 .)

steps.title: Pierce BCA Protein Assay Kit #23225...
steps.instruction: Pierce BCA Protein Assay Kit #23225 or #23227, Thermo Sci with 2 mg/ml BSA standard. Use instructions from kit. Equilibrate reagents, samples, and standards to Room temperature . Use the Microplate reader instructions.

steps.title: Prepare a dilution series of BSA...
steps.instruction: Prepare a dilution series of BSA in working range of 20 μg/ml – 2000 μg/ml from 1 glass vial of stock 2 mg/ml BSA in the kit for the standard curve: %s %s

steps.title: Prepare fresh working reagent 50:1 of...
steps.instruction: Prepare fresh working reagent 50:1 of A:B, enough for all standards, unknowns, and replicates of the unknowns (should have n=3).

steps.title: Add 25 µL of unknown or...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL of unknown or standard to 96-well plate well followed by 200 µL, working reagent using 200 μl pipettor and gently mix avoiding spillover .

steps.title: Cover plate with foil and incubate...
steps.instruction: Cover plate with foil and incubate 37 °C for 00:30:00 in shaking incubator, gently shaking.

steps.title: Cool to Room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool to Room temperature .

steps.title: Measure Absorbance on the VarioSkan at 562 nm
steps.instruction: Measure Absorbance on the VarioSkan at 562 nm.

steps.title: Determine protein concentration of your unknown...
steps.instruction: Determine protein concentration of your unknown from the standard curve (the Skanit software will plot the curve and the unknowns on it if you edit the standards in the plate layout to include the concentrations above in the table.)

steps.title: Prepare the following solutions for SDS-PAGE,...
steps.instruction: Prepare the following solutions for SDS-PAGE, Transfer, and Antibody Incubations: %s %s %s %s %s %s %s %s

steps.title: In a fume hood, Add 100...
steps.instruction: In a fume hood, Add 100 µL, 2-mercaptoethanol to 900 µL, 4x Laemmli sample buffer to make MLB.

steps.title: Pre-soak blotting stacks in 1X transfer...
steps.instruction: Pre-soak blotting stacks in 1X transfer buffer made according to directions on the bottle.

steps.title: Pre-wet PVDF membrane from Biorad Transblot...
steps.instruction: Pre-wet PVDF membrane from Biorad Transblot kit in 190 proof ethanol, then in transfer buffer.

steps.title: Layer into Transblot drawer (stack, membrane,...
steps.instruction: Layer into Transblot drawer (stack, membrane, gel, second stack) before rolling out bubbles gently with conical tube

steps.title: Run the transfer on Biorad setting,...
steps.instruction: Run the transfer on Biorad setting, standard Mininigel TGX, 25mA, 25V, 00:03:00 .

steps.title: While running, pour 5% milk block...
steps.instruction: While running, pour 5% milk block in 1X PBST directly into cleaned small black Western blot box.

steps.title: Incubate transferred blot for 01:00:00 at...
steps.instruction: Incubate transferred blot for 01:00:00 at Room temperature in 5% milk block in 1X PBST.

steps.title: Photograph the blot after the transfer...
steps.instruction: Photograph the blot after the transfer to help keep track of orientation.

steps.title: Primary and secondary antibody binding β-Actin (C4)
steps.instruction: Primary and secondary antibody binding β-Actin (C4): sc-47778 ECL SuperSignal West Pico Chemiluminescent Substrate Use 1:500 Dilutions for primary antibodies, in 5% milk/PBST Note: Primary antibody ( unconjugated with HRP ) can be re-used 3x if kept frozen.

steps.title: Actin staining with anti-actin-HRP primary Wash...
steps.instruction: Actin staining with anti-actin-HRP primary Wash blot 3x with 1X PBST < 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Imaging Blot on BioRad ChemiDoc XRS+...
steps.instruction: Imaging Blot on BioRad ChemiDoc XRS+ (Vollmers lab) Place blot in between sheet protector plastic and transport to imager in cassette to keep dark.

steps.title: Export report as an excel doc
steps.instruction: Export report as an excel doc.

steps.title: Dilute samples with MLB at a...
steps.instruction: Dilute samples with MLB at a ratio of 3 parts sample to 1 part MLB. So for 30 µL final volume, add 7.5 µL, MLB to 22.5 µL, sample in RIPA in 1.5 ml LoBind safety lock tube.

steps.title: Denature at 95 °C for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Denature at 95 °C for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Transfer to ice
steps.instruction: Transfer to ice.

steps.title: Remove gel from package, take off...
steps.instruction: Remove gel from package, take off green strip at the bottom, and rinse the wells three times with about 1 mL, running buffer (use 1 ml pipettor).

steps.title: Place gel, tall plate facing out...
steps.instruction: Place gel, tall plate facing out in outer side of a holder and the reservoir block at the other to make a running buffer reservoir. Put the other gel holder in place to take up space.

steps.title: Fill the chamber to the 2-gel mark
steps.instruction: Fill the chamber to the 2-gel mark. Make sure there are no leaks before testing the circuit. Test the circuit by checking for bubbles after turning on power to 70 volts .

steps.title: Using a 20 ul conte-tipped pipettor,...
steps.instruction: Using a 20 ul conte-tipped pipettor, load 30 µL of the samples in MLB onto gel, as well as 10 µL of Biorad Precision Plus protein ladder Dual Color.

steps.title: Run at 100 – 120V, depending...
steps.instruction: Run at 100 – 120V, depending on desired resolution, until adequate separation of ladder lanes in sizes regions of interest.

steps.title: Photograph the gel after the run...
steps.instruction: Photograph the gel after the run with your phone to help keep track of orientation.

steps.title: Drain milk block from blots and...
steps.instruction: Drain milk block from blots and add primary Ab (in milk block).

steps.title: Incubate in cold room Overnight covered...
steps.instruction: Incubate in cold room Overnight covered and shaking on orbital shaker, covered with plastic.

steps.title: The next day , wash blot...
steps.instruction: The next day , wash blot 3x / 00:15:00 in PBST.

steps.title: Freshly prepare secondary antibody at 1:10,000...
steps.instruction: Freshly prepare secondary antibody at 1:10,000 dilution ( 2 µL to 20 mL, 5% milk/PBST ).

steps.title: Incubate in secondary antibody for 01:00:00 , Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate in secondary antibody for 01:00:00 , Room temperature .

steps.title: Discard secondary and wash blot 3x / 00:05:00 in 1xPBST
steps.instruction: Discard secondary and wash blot 3x / 00:05:00 in 1xPBST.

steps.title: Discard secondary and in the same...
steps.instruction: Discard secondary and in the same box or moving the blot to a new box, add ECL working solution ( 1 mL of both reagents mixed in a 15 mL conical and applied to blot.)

steps.title: Incubate in ECL for 00:05:00 ,...
steps.instruction: Incubate in ECL for 00:05:00 , shaking occasionally by hand, covered.

steps.title: Drain blot and place between plastic...
steps.instruction: Drain blot and place between plastic sheets for imaging using Biorad GelDoc, as below. After imaging, the blot can be probed for actin as a protein loading control without stripping the blot, using anti-actin-HRP as described below.

steps.title: Incubate 00:20:00 , Room temperature in...
steps.instruction: Incubate 00:20:00 , Room temperature in the primary actin antibody 1:500 freshly made dilution in 5% Milk block.

steps.title: Wash 2 – 3x in 1x PBST 00:05:00 each
steps.instruction: Wash 2 – 3x in 1x PBST 00:05:00 each.

steps.title: Incubate in ECL as above (step 51) and image
steps.instruction: Incubate in ECL as above (step 51) and image.

steps.title: Place blot on white screen
steps.instruction: Place blot on white screen.

steps.title: Place screen into chamber drawer
steps.instruction: Place screen into chamber drawer.

steps.title: Select new protocol, then blot, then...
steps.instruction: Select new protocol, then blot, then chem hi sensitivity and position blot under live focus setting.

steps.title: Under Applications, select chemidoc hi sensitivity to photograph target bands
steps.instruction: Under Applications, select chemidoc hi sensitivity to photograph target bands. Note the image size, the gain (2X), and bin (2x2) to make sure the image size is the same when the protein standards are photographed in order to be able to merge the images.

steps.title: Under Live Acquire, select acquisition settings
steps.instruction: Under Live Acquire, select acquisition settings: 1, 600, for range and total =100 acquisitions (the 100 can be lowered for longer exposure), starting at 0.25 start time for high expression target.

steps.title: Freeze and save the image of...
steps.instruction: Freeze and save the image of your choice as it comes up.

steps.title: Without moving the blot , take...
steps.instruction: Without moving the blot , take a picture to visualize the protein standards by selecting Custom under Applications, then create an Epi illumination protocol with the same gain and binning as used for photographing the bands (double check the image size prediction under the options…)

steps.title: Merge the target bands and the...
steps.instruction: Merge the target bands and the protein standards pictures in Image Lab software using Image tools. Note: Imaged blots can be stored at 4ºC in PBST for stripping and re-probing. Some folks also store them frozen in -20ºC flat in plastic bag.
